          </div>\n793\t        </div>\n794\t    </div>\n795\t\n796\t    <!-- Render helpers (createTopicCard + friends) — loaded before the app\n797\t         script so they're available as globals generateId() {\n819\t            return 'adc-' + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 16);\n820\t        }\n821\t\n822\t        // createTopicCard / escapeHtml / formatStaleness / getStalenessLevel live\n823\t        // in /canvas.js (loaded below as a blocking <script src> before this\n824\t        // block), so they are available as globals here and unit-testable\n825\t        // headlessly. document.getElementById('statusText');\n830\t\n831\t            dot.className = 'status-dot ' + status;\n832\t            statusText.textContent = text;\n833\t        }\n834\t\n835\t        function showWorkloadSummary(summary) {\n836\t            const el = document.getElementById('workloadSummary');\n837\t            const parts = [];\n838\t\n839\t            if (summary.pending_intents > 0) {\n840\t                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.pending_intents}</strong> pending intents`);\n841\t            }\n842\t            if (summary.new_results > 0) {\n843\t                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.new_results}</strong> new results`);\n844\t            }\n845\t            if (summary.unresolved_exceptions > 0) {\n846\t                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.unresolved_exceptions}</strong> unresolved exceptions`);\n847\t            }\n848\t\n849\t            if (parts.length > 0) {\n850\t                el.innerHTML = '📊 While you were away: ' + parts.join(', ');\n851\t                el.classList.add('show');\n852\t            } else {\n853\t                el.classList.remove('show');\n854\t            }\n855\t        }\n856\t\n857\t        async function registerSurface() {\n858\t            try {\n859\t                const response = await fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/register', {\n860\t                    method: 'POST',\n861\t                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n862\t                    body: JSON.stringify({\n863\t                        session_id: sessionId,\n864\t                        surface_type: surfaceType\n865\t                    })\n866\t                });\n867\t\n868\t                if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed to register surface');\n869\t\n870\t                const data = await response.json();\n871\t                surfaceId = data.surface_id;\n872\t                return surfaceId;\n873\t            } catch (error) {\n874\t                console.error('Error registering surface:', error);\n875\t                throw error;\n876\t            }\n877\t        }\n878\t\n879\t        // Registry project list for the first-run welcome card\n880\t        // (config/registry.yaml → GET /api/v1/registry). DB/result independent —\n881\t        // the welcome card renders even against an empty component store. Cached\n882\t        // after the first fetch so the per-SSE-event reloads don't refetch it.\n883\t        let welcomeProjects = null;\n884\t\n885\t        async function loadWelcomeProjects() {\n886\t            if (welcomeProjects) return welcomeProjects;\n887\t            try {\n888\t                const resp = await fetch('/api/v1/registry');\n889\t                if (resp.ok) {\n890\t                    const data = await resp.json();\n891\t                    welcomeProjects = (data && data.projects) || [];\n892\t                    return welcomeProjects;\n893\t                }\n894\t            } catch (error) {\n895\t                console.error('Error loading registry for welcome card:', error);\n896\t            }\n897\t            // Graceful fallback: an empty list still renders the welcome card\n898\t            // (with default example utterances), so a wedged registry endpoint\n899\t            // never leaves the canvas bare.\n900\t            welcomeProjects = [];\n901\t            return welcomeProjects;\n902\t        }\n903\t\n904\t        async function loadTopics() {\n905\t            try {\n906\t                const response = await fetch(`/api/v1/sessions/${sessionId}/topics`);\n907\t                if (!response.ok) return;\n908\t\n909\t                const data = await response.json();\n910\t                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n911\t                const cards = data.cards || [];\n912\t\n913\t                // Fresh session (zero topics): show the first-run welcome card\n914\t                // instead of the bare empty-state. Its project list + example\n915\t                // utterances come from the registry (never the DB), so we only\n916\t                // need to fetch it on the zero-card branch. The welcome card is\n917\t                // dropped — not duplicated — the moment the first real result\n918\t                // lands: the next SSE result_created → loadTopics() reload\n919\t                // yields a non-empty card set, so buildContainerChildren()\n920\t                // returns topic cards only (clearing the welcome card).\n921\t                const projects = cards.length ? [] : await loadWelcomeProjects();\n922\t\n923\t                container.innerHTML = '';\n924\t                buildContainerChildren(cards, projects).forEach(node => {\n925\t                    container.appendChild(node);\n926\t                });\n927\t            } catch (error) {\n928\t                console.error('Error loading topics:', error);\n929\t            }\n930\t        }\n931\t\n932\t        function connectSSE() {\n933\t            updateConnectionStatus('connecting', 'Connecting...');\n934\t\n935\t            const url = `/api/v1/sse?surface_id=${encodeURIComponent(surfaceId)}&session_id=${encodeURIComponent(sessionId)}&surface_type=${encodeURIComponent(surfaceType)}`;\n936\t            eventSource = new EventSource(url);\n937\t\n938\t            eventSource.onopen = () => {\n939\t                updateConnectionStatus('', 'Connected');\n940\t                loadTopics();\n941\t            };\n942\t\n943\t            eventSource.onerror = (error) => {\n944\t                console.error('SSE error:', error);\n945\t                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Disconnected');\n946\t            };\n947\t\n948\t            eventSource.addEventListener('connected', (event) => {\n949\t                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n950\t                console.log('SSE connected:', data);\n951\t            });\n952\t\n953\t            eventSource.addEventListener('workload_summary', (event) => {\n954\t                const summary = JSON.parse(event.data);\n955\t                showWorkloadSummary(summary);\n956\t            });\n957\t\n958\t            // Dispatch ack: backup path for intent_ids if the fetch response didn't\n959\t            // include them (e.g., the response arrived before the intent was routed).\n960\t            eventSource.addEventListener('dispatch_ack', (event) => {\n961\t                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n962\t                const utteranceId = data.utterance_id;\n963\t                const intentIds = data.intent_ids;\n964\t                const utterance = data.utterance;\n965\t\n966\t                if (!utteranceId || !intentIds || !Array.isArray(intentIds)) return;\n967\t\n968\t                // Check if there's an existing placeholder to split\n969\t                const placeholder = pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId];\n970\t                const createdAt = placeholder\n971\t                    ? parseInt(placeholder.dataset.createdAt, 10)\n972\t                    : Date.now();\n973\t\n974\t                // Split into per-thread pending cards\n975\t                const threadCards = splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds);\n976\t\n977\t                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n978\t\n979\t                if (placeholder && placeholder.parentNode) {\n980\t                    // Replace the placeholder with the thread cards\n981\t                    const frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n982\t                    threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n983\t                    placeholder.parentNode.replaceChild(frag, placeholder);\n984\t                } else {\n985\t                    // No placeholder (test scenario or race condition): prepend thread cards\n986\t                    const frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n987\t                    threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n988\t                    container.insertBefore(frag, container.firstChild);\n989\t                }\n990\t\n991\t                // No longer track this placeholder\n992\t                delete pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId];\n993\t            });\n994\t\n995\t            // Thread-level progress event\n996\t            eventSource.addEventListener('thread_progress', (event) => {\n997\t                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n998\t                const threadId = data.thread_id;\n999\t\n1000\t                if (!threadId) {\n1001\t                    console.warn('thread_progress: missing thread_id in event data');\n1002\t                    return;\n1003\t                }\n1004\t\n1005\t                // Find the pending thread card by thread_id\n1006\t                const threadCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + threadId + '\"]');\n1007\t                if (!threadCard) {\n1008\t                    console.warn('thread_progress: threadCard not found for thread_id=' + threadId);\n1009\t                    return;\n1010\t                }\n1011\t                if (!threadCard.classList.contains('pending-card')) {\n1012\t                    console.warn('thread_progress: threadCard does not have pending-card class for thread_id=' + threadId);\n1013\t                    return;\n1014\t                }\n1015\t                if (typeof _setProgress !== 'function') {\n1016\t                    console.warn('thread_progress: _setProgress is not a function');\n1017\t                    return;\n1018\t                }\n1019\t\n1020\t                // Pass progress message (e.g., '3/5 sources in') to _setProgress()\n1021\t                const completed = data.completed;\n1022\t                const total = data.total;\n1023\t                _setProgress(threadCard, { completed: completed, total: total });\n1024\t            });\n1025\t\n1026\t            // Per-source progress update for a thread\n1027\t            eventSource.addEventListener('progress_update', (event) => {\n1028\t                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n1029\t                const intentId = data.intent_id;\n1030\t                const completed = data.completed;\n1031\t                const total = data.total;\n1032\t\n1033\t                if (!intentId) return;\n1034\t\n1035\t                // Find the pending thread card by intent_id\n1036\t                const threadCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]');\n1037\t                if (!threadCard) {\n1038\t                    console.warn('progress_update: threadCard is null for intent_id=' + intentId);\n1039\t                    return;\n1040\t                }\n1041\t                if (!threadCard.classList.contains('pending-card')) {\n1042\t                    console.warn('progress_update: threadCard does not have pending-card class for intent_id=' + intentId);\n1043\t                    return;\n1044\t                }\n1045\t                if (typeof _setProgress !== 'function') {\n1046\t                    console.warn('progress_update: _setProgress is not a function');\n1047\t                    return;\n1048\t                }\n1049\t                _setProgress(threadCard, { completed: completed, total: total });\n1050\t            });\n1051\t\n1052\t            eventSource.addEventListener('result_created', (event) => {\n1053\t                const result = JSON.parse(event.data);\n1054\t                console.log('New result:', result);\n1055\t\n1056\t                // Find and remove the pending card for this intent before reloading\n1057\t                const intentId = result.intent_id;\n1058\t                if (intentId) {\n1059\t                    const pendingCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]');\n1060\t                    if (pendingCard) {\n1061\t                        // Remove the pending card so it doesn't flicker when loadTopics() renders the real result\n1062\t      while (cur && cur !== document.body && cur !== document.documentElement) {\n1485\t                if (cur.id) { parts.unshift('#' + cur.id); break; }\n1486\t                var tag =     window.history.replaceState({}, '', `${window.location.pathname}?${urlParams}`);\n        }\n\n        function generateId() {\n            return 'adc-' + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 16);\n        }\n\n        // createTopicCard / escapeHtml / formatStaleness / getStalenessLevel live\n        // in /canvas.js (loaded below as a blocking <script src> before this\n        // block), so they are available as globals here and unit-testable\n        // headlessly. See src/canvas/canvas.js.\n\n        function updateConnectionStatus(status, text) {\n            const dot = document.getElementById('statusDot');\n            const statusText = document.getElementById('statusText');\n\n            dot.className = 'status-dot ' + status;\n            statusText.textContent = text;\n        }\n\n        function showWorkloadSummary(summary) {\n            const el = document.getElementById('workloadSummary');\n            const parts = [];\n\n            if (summary.pending_intents > 0) {\n                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.pending_intents}</strong> pending intents`);\n            }\n            if (summary.new_results > 0) {\n                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.new_results}</strong> new results`);\n            }\n            if (summary.unresolved_exceptions > 0) {\n                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.unresolved_exceptions}</strong> unresolved exceptions`);\n            }\n\n            if (parts.length > 0) {\n                el.innerHTML = '📊 While you were away: ' + parts.join(', ');\n                el.classList.add('show');\n            } else {\n                el.classList.remove('show');\n            }\n        }\n\n        async function registerSurface() {\n            try {\n                const response = await fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/register', {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                    body: JSON.stringify({\n                        session_id: sessionId,\n                        surface_type: surfaceType\n                    })\n                });\n\n                if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed to register surface');\n\n                const data = await response.json();\n                surfaceId = data.surface_id;\n                return surfaceId;\n            } catch (error) {\n                console.error('Error registering surface:', error);\n                throw error;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Registry project list for the first-run welcome card\n        // (config/registry.yaml → GET /api/v1/registry). DB/result independent —\n        // the welcome card renders even against an empty component store. Cached\n        // after the first fetch so the per-SSE-event reloads don't refetch it.\n        let welcomeProjects = null;\n\n        async function loadWelcomeProjects() {\n            if (welcomeProjects) return welcomeProjects;\n            try {\n                const resp = await fetch('/api/v1/registry');\n                if (resp.ok) {\n                    const data = await resp.json();\n                    welcomeProjects = (data && data.projects) || [];\n                    return welcomeProjects;\n                }\n            } catch (error) {\n                console.error('Error loading registry for welcome card:', error);\n            }\n            // Graceful fallback: an empty list still renders the welcome card\n            // (with default example utterances), so a wedged registry endpoint\n            // never leaves the canvas bare.\n            welcomeProjects = [];\n            return welcomeProjects;\n        }\n\n        async function loadTopics() {\n            try {\n                const response = await fetch(`/api/v1/sessions/${sessionId}/topics`);\n                if (!response.ok) return;\n\n                const data = await response.json();\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n                const cards = data.cards || [];\n\n                // Fresh session (zero topics): show the first-run welcome card\n                // instead of the bare empty-state. Its project list + example\n                // utterances come from the registry (never the DB), so we only\n                // need to fetch it on the zero-card branch. The welcome card is\n                // dropped — not duplicated — the moment the first real result\n                // lands: the next SSE result_created → loadTopics() reload\n                // yields a non-empty card set, so buildContainerChildren()\n                // returns topic cards only (clearing the welcome card).\n                const projects = cards.length ? [] : await loadWelcomeProjects();\n\n                container.innerHTML = '';\n                buildContainerChildren(cards, projects).forEach(node => {\n                    container.appendChild(node);\n                });\n            } catch (error) {\n                console.error('Error loading topics:', error);\n            }\n        }\n\n        function connectSSE() {\n            updateConnectionStatus('connecting', 'Connecting...');\n\n            const url = `/api/v1/sse?surface_id=${encodeURIComponent(surfaceId)}&session_id=${encodeURIComponent(sessionId)}&surface_type=${encodeURIComponent(surfaceType)}`;\n            eventSource = new EventSource(url);\n\n            eventSource.onopen = () => {\n                updateConnectionStatus('', 'Connected');\n                loadTopics();\n            };\n\n            eventSource.onerror = (error) => {\n                console.error('SSE error:', error);\n                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Disconnected');\n            };\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('connected', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('SSE connected:', data);\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('workload_summary', (event) => {\n                const summary = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                showWorkloadSummary(summary);\n            });\n\n            // Dispatch ack: backup path for intent_ids if the fetch response didn't\n            // include them (e.g., the response arrived before the intent was routed).\n            eventSource.addEventListener('dispatch_ack', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                const utteranceId = data.utterance_id;\n                const intentIds = data.intent_ids;\n                const utterance = data.utterance;\n\n                if (!utteranceId || !intentIds || !Array.isArray(intentIds)) return;\n\n                // Check if there's an existing placeholder to split\n                const placeholder = pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId];\n                const createdAt = placeholder\n                    ? parseInt(placeholder.dataset.createdAt, 10)\n                    : Date.now();\n\n                // Split into per-thread pending cards\n                const threadCards = splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds);\n\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n\n                if (placeholder && placeholder.parentNode) {\n                    // Replace the placeholder with the thread cards\n                    const frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n                    threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n                    placeholder.parentNode.replaceChild(frag, placeholder);\n                } else {\n                    // No placeholder (test scenario or race condition): prepend thread cards\n                    const frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n                    threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n                    container.insertBefore(frag, container.firstChild);\n                }\n\n                // No longer track this placeholder\n                delete pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId];\n            });\n\n            // Thread-level progress event\n            eventSource.addEventListener('thread_progress', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                const threadId = data.thread_id;\n\n                if (!threadId) {\n                    console.warn('thread_progress: missing thread_id in event data');\n                    return;\n                }\n\n                // Find the pending thread card by thread_id\n                const threadCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + threadId + '\"]');\n                if (!threadCard) {\n                    console.warn('thread_progress: threadCard not found for thread_id=' + threadId);\n                    return;\n                }\n                if (!threadCard.classList.contains('pending-card')) {\n                    console.warn('thread_progress: threadCard does not have pending-card class for thread_id=' + threadId);\n                    return;\n                }\n                if (typeof _setProgress !== 'function') {\n                    console.warn('thread_progress: _setProgress is not a function');\n                    return;\n                }\n\n                // Pass progress message (e.g., '3/5 sources in') to _setProgress()\n                const completed = data.completed;\n                const total = data.total;\n                _setProgress(threadCard, { completed: completed, total: total });\n            });\n\n            // Per-source progress update for a thread\n            eventSource.addEventListener('progress_update', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                const intentId = data.intent_id;\n                const completed = data.completed;\n                const total = data.total;\n\n                if (!intentId) return;\n\n                // Find the pending thread card by intent_id\n                const threadCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]');\n                if (!threadCard) {\n                    console.warn('progress_update: threadCard is null for intent_id=' + intentId);\n                    return;\n                }\n                if (!threadCard.classList.contains('pending-card')) {\n                    console.warn('progress_update: threadCard does not have pending-card class for intent_id=' + intentId);\n                    return;\n                }\n                if (typeof _setProgress !== 'function') {\n                    console.warn('progress_update: _setProgress is not a function');\n                    return;\n                }\n                _setProgress(threadCard, { completed: completed, total: total });\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('result_created', (event) => {\n                const result = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('New result:', result);\n\n                // Find and remove the pending card for this intent before reloading\n                const intentId = result.intent_id;\n                if (intentId) {\n                    const pendingCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]');\n                    if (pendingCard) {\n                        // Remove the pending card so it doesn't flicker when loadTopics() renders the real result\n                        pendingCard.remove();\n                        console.log('Removed pending card for intent_id:', intentId);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                loadTopics(); // Reload topics to show new result\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('topic_updated', (event) => {\n                const topic = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Topic updated:', topic);\n                loadTopics();\n            });\n\n            // Task stuck event: render stuck card when a bead is fenced\n            eventSource.addEventListener('task_stuck', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Task stuck:', data);\n\n                // Find and remove any pending card for this intent\n                const intentId = data.intent_id;\n                if (intentId) {\n                    const pendingCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]');\n                    if (pendingCard) {\n                        pendingCard.remove();\n                        console.log('Removed pending card for stuck intent_id:', intentId);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Create and prepend the stuck card\n                const stuckCard = createStuckCard(data);\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n\n                // Clear loading/empty state if present\n                if (container.querySelector('.loading') || container.querySelector('.empty-state')) {\n                    container.innerHTML = '';\n                }\n\n                // Prepend stuck card (newest first)\n                container.insertBefore(stuckCard, container.firstChild);\n            });\n\n            // Task failed event: render failed card on terminal failure\n            eventSource.addEventListener('task_failed', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Task failed:', data);\n\n                // Find and remove any pending card for this intent\n                const intentId = data.intent_id;\n                if (intentId) {\n                    const pendingCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]');\n                    if (pendingCard) {\n                        pendingCard.remove();\n                        console.log('Removed pending card for failed intent_id:', intentId);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Create and prepend the failed card\n                const failedCard = createFailedCard(data);\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n\n                // Clear loading/empty state if present\n                if (container.querySelector('.loading') || container.querySelector('.empty-state')) {\n                    container.innerHTML = '';\n                }\n\n                // Prepend failed card (newest first)\n                container.insertBefore(failedCard, container.firstChild);\n            });\n\n            // Error event: render error card from degraded-state SSE error events\n            eventSource.addEventListener('error', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Error event:', data);\n\n                // Find and remove any pending card for this intent/pending before rendering error\n                const intentId = data.intent_id;\n                const pendingId = data.pending_id;\n                let selector = null;\n                if (intentId) {\n                    selector = '[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]';\n                } else if (pendingId) {\n                    selector = '[data-pending-id=\"' + pendingId + '\"]';\n                }\n\n                if (selector) {\n                    const pendingCard = document.querySelector(selector);\n                    if (pendingCard) {\n                        pendingCard.remove();\n                        console.log('Removed pending card for error event:', selector);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Create and prepend the error card\n                const errorCard = createErrorCard(data);\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n\n                // Clear loading/empty state if present\n                if (container.querySelector('.loading') || container.querySelector('.empty-state')) {\n                    container.innerHTML = '';\n                }\n\n                // Prepend error card (newest first)\n                container.insertBefore(errorCard, container.firstChild);\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('disconnect', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Server disconnect:', data);\n                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Disconnected');\n                eventSource.close();\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Elapsed-time ticker for pending cards\n        // Every second, update the elapsed-time footer on all pending cards\n        // and apply aged treatment if they've exceeded the 30s threshold.\n        setInterval(function () {\n            var now = Date.now();\n            var pendingCards = document.querySelectorAll('.pending-card[data-pending-id]');\n            pendingCards.forEach(function (card) {\n                tickPendingElapsed(card, now);\n                applyAgedTreatment(card, now);\n            });\n        }, 1000);\n\n        // Heartbeat to keep surface alive\n        setInterval(() => {\n            if (surfaceId) {\n                fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/heartbeat', {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                    body: JSON.stringify({\n                        surface_id: surfaceId,\n                        session_id: sessionId\n                    })\n                }).catch(console.error);\n            }\n        }, 30000); // Every 30 seconds\n\n        // ── Input bar ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n        const textarea = document.getElementById('inputTextarea');\n        const btnSend  = document.getElementById('btnSend');\n        const btnMic   = document.getElementById('btnMic');\n\n        // Auto-resize textarea\n        textarea.addEventListener('input', () => {\n            textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n            textarea.style.height = Math.min(textarea.scrollHeight, 160) + 'px';\n            btnSend.disabled = textarea.value.trim().length === 0;\n        });\n\n        // Send on Enter (Shift+Enter = newline)\n        textarea.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {\n            if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) {\n                e.preventDefault();\n                if (!btnSend.disabled) dispatch(textarea.value.trim());\n            }\n        });\n\n        btnSend.addEventListener('click', () => {\n            if (!btnSend.disabled) dispatch(textarea.value.trim());\n        });\n\n        async function dispatch(utterance) {\n            if (!utterance) return;\n            textarea.value = '';\n            textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n            btnSend.disabled = true;\n\n            // Generate a client-side pending ID for the placeholder. We'll swap it\n            // for the server's utterance_id when the response arrives.\n            var pendingId = 'pending-' + Date.now() + '-' + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 8);\n            var createdAt = Date.now();\n\n            // Create the pending placeholder NOW, before the server round-trip,\n            // so a hung server still leaves a card on canvas to age.\n            var placeholder = createPendingPlaceholderCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId);\n            var container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n\n            // Clear the empty-state/loading spinner if present\n            if (container.querySelector('.loading') || container.querySelector('.empty-state')) {\n                container.innerHTML = '';\n            }\n\n            // Prepend the placeholder (newest first)\n            container.insertBefore(placeholder, container.firstChild);\n\n            // Track it so we can find/replace it later\n            pendingPlaceholders[pendingId] = placeholder;\n\n            try {\n                var resp = await fetch('/dispatch', {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                    body: JSON.stringify({ utterance, session_id: sessionId, surface_id: surfaceId, pending_id: pendingId }),\n                });\n\n                if (!resp.ok) {\n                    var errText = await resp.text();\n                    console.error('Dispatch error:', errText);\n                    // Try to parse as JSON for structured error\n                    var errData;\n                    try { errData = JSON.parse(errText); } catch (e) {}\n                    if (errData && errData.error_type) {\n                        // Replace placeholder with error card\n                        var errorCard = createErrorCard(errData);\n                        if (placeholder && placeholder.parentNode) {\n                            placeholder.parentNode.replaceChild(errorCard, placeholder);\n                        }\n                        delete pendingPlaceholders[pendingId];\n                    }\n                    return;\n                }\n\n                var data = await resp.json();\n                // Swap to the server's utterance_id for consistency\n                var utteranceId = data.utterance_id || pendingId;\n                placeholder.dataset.pendingId = utteranceId;\n\n                // Move the placeholder tracking to the new key\n                delete pendingPlaceholders[pendingId];\n                pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId] = placeholder;\n\n                // If intent_ids are present, split into per-thread pending cards\n                if (data.intent_ids && Array.isArray(data.intent_ids) && data.intent_ids.length > 0) {\n                    var threadCards = splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, data.intent_ids);\n                    // Replace the placeholder with the thread cards\n                    if (placeholder && placeholder.parentNode) {\n                        var frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n                        threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n                        placeholder.parentNode.replaceChild(frag, placeholder);\n                    }\n                    delete pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId];\n                }\n            } catch (err) {\n                console.error('Dispatch failed:', err);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Web Speech API mic\n        let recognition = null;\n\n        if ('webkitSpeechRecognition' in window || 'SpeechRecognition' in window) {\n            const SpeechRecognition = window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition;\n            recognition = new SpeechRecognition();\n            recognition.continuous = false;\n            recognition.interimResults = true;\n            recognition.lang = 'en-US';\n\n            let finalTranscript = '';\n\n            recognition.onstart = () => {\n                btnMic.classList.add('recording');\n                finalTranscript = textarea.value;\n            };\n\n            recognition.onresult = (e) => {\n                let interim = '';\n                for (let i = e.resultIndex; i < e.results.length; i++) {\n                    if (e.results[i].isFinal) {\n                        finalTranscript += e.results[i][0].transcript;\n                    } else {\n                        interim += e.results[i][0].transcript;\n                    }\n                }\n                textarea.value = finalTranscript + interim;\n                textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n                textarea.style.height = Math.min(textarea.scrollHeight, 160) + 'px';\n                btnSend.disabled = textarea.value.trim().length === 0;\n            };\n\n            recognition.onend = () => {\n                btnMic.classList.remove('recording');\n                if (finalTranscript.trim()) dispatch(finalTranscript.trim());\n            };\n\n            recognition.onerror = (e) => {\n                console.error('Speech recognition error:', e.error);\n                btnMic.classList.remove('recording');\n            };\n\n            btnMic.addEventListener('click', () => {\n                if (btnMic.classList.contains('recording')) {\n                    recognition.stop();\n                } else {\n                    recognition.start();\n                }\n            });\n        } else {\n            // Fallback: MediaRecorder + whisper-stt for browsers without Web Speech API\n            let mediaRecorder = null;\n            let audioChunks = [];\n\n            async function startRecording() {\n                try {\n                    const stream = await navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: true });\n                    mediaRecorder = new MediaRecorder(stream);\n                    audioChunks = [];\n\n                    mediaRecorder.ondataavailable = (event) => {\n                        if (event.data.size > 0) {\n                            audioChunks.push(event.data);\n                        }\n                    };\n\n                    mediaRecorder.onstop = async () => {\n                        btnMic.classList.remove('recording');\n                        btnMic.innerHTML = '🎤';\n\n                        // Create audio blob and convert to base64\n                        const audioBlob = new Blob(audioChunks, { type: 'audio/webm' });\n                        const reader = new FileReader();\n                        reader.onloadend = async () => {\n                            const base64Audio = reader.result.split(',')[1];\n\n                            try {\n                                const resp = await fetch('/api/v1/stt', {\n                                    method: 'POST',\n                                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                                    body: JSON.stringify({\n                                        audio_data: base64Audio,\n                                        format: 'webm'\n                                    })\n                                });\n\n                                if (resp.ok) {\n                                    const data = await resp.json();\n                                    if (data.text) {\n                                        textarea.value = data.text;\n                                        textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n                                        textarea.style.height = Math.min(textarea.scrollHeight, 160) + 'px';\n                                        btnSend.disabled = textarea.value.trim().length === 0;\n                                        // Auto-dispatch on successful transcription\n                                        dispatch(data.text.trim());\n                                    }\n                                } else {\n                                    const err = await resp.json();\n                                    console.error('STT error:', err);\n                                    btnMic.title = `Transcription failed: ${err.error || 'Unknown error'}`;\n                                    setTimeout(() => {\n                                        btnMic.title = 'Click to record (whisper-stt fallback)';\n                                    }, 3000);\n                                }\n                            } catch (err) {\n                                console.error('STT request failed:', err);\n                                btnMic.title = 'STT service unavailable';\n                                setTimeout(() => {\n                                    btnMic.title = 'Click to record (whisper-stt fallback)';\n                                }, 3000);\n                            }\n                        };\n                        reader.readAsDataURL(audioBlob);\n\n                        // Stop all tracks to release microphone\n                        stream.getTracks().forEach(track => track.stop());\n                    };\n\n                    mediaRecorder.start();\n                    btnMic.classList.add('recording');\n                    btnMic.innerHTML = '⏹';\n                } catch (err) {\n                    console.error('MediaRecorder access failed:', err);\n                    btnMic.title = 'Microphone access denied';\n                    btnMic.style.opacity = '0.4';\n                    btnMic.disabled = true;\n                }\n            }\n\n            function stopRecording() {\n                if (mediaRecorder && mediaRecorder.state === 'recording') {\n                    mediaRecorder.stop();\n                }\n            }\n\n            btnMic.title = 'Click to record (whisper-stt fallback)';\n            btnMic.disabled = false;\n            btnMic.style.opacity = '1';\n\n            btnMic.addEventListener('click', () => {\n                if (btnMic.classList.contains('recording')) {\n                    stopRecording();\n                } else {\n                    startRecording();\n                }\n            });\n        }\n\n        // ── Initialize ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n        // Initialize\n        async function init() {\n            try {\n                // Show version\n                const meta = await fetch('/openapi.json').then(r => r.json()).catch(() => null);\n                if (meta && meta.info && meta.info.version) {\n                    document.getElementById('versionBadge').textContent = 'v' + meta.info.version;\n                }\n\n                await registerSurface();\n                connectSSE();\n            } catch (error) {\n                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Failed to connect');\n                console.error('Initialization error:', error);\n            }\n        }\n\n        init();\n    </script>\n\n    <!-- ── Agentation: element-selector feedback toolbar ────────────────────── -->\n    <script>\n    (function () {\n        var active = false;\n        var selectedEl = null;\n\n        // Build a stable CSS selector for a given element\n        function buildSelector(el) {\n            if (!el || el === document.body) return 'body';\n            var parts = [];\n            var cur = el;\n            while (cur && cur !== document.body && cur !== document.documentElement) {\n                if (cur.id) { parts.unshift('#' + cur.id); break; }\n                var tag = cur.tagName.toLowerCase();\n                var classes = Array.from(cur.classList)\n                    .filter(function (c) { return c.length > 1; })\n                    .slice(0, 2);\n                var part = tag + (classes.length ? '.' + classes.join('.') : '');\n                // add nth-of-type when siblings share the same tag\n                if (cur.parentElement) {\n                    var sibs = Array.from(cur.parentElement.children)\n                        .filter(function (s) { return s.tagName === cur.tagName; });\n                    if (sibs.length > 1) part += ':nth-of-type(' + (sibs.indexOf(cur) + 1) + ')';\n                }\n                parts.unshift(part);\n                cur = cur.parentElement;\n            }\n            return parts.join(' > ');\n        }\n\n        // Highlight box\n        var hlBox = document.createElement('div');\n        hlBox.style.cssText = [\n            'position:fixed', 'pointer-events:none', 'z-index:9998',\n            'border:2px solid #3b82f6', 'background:rgba(59,130,246,.08)',\n            'border-radius:4px', 'display:none', 'transition:all .08s'\n        ].join(';');\n        document.body.appendChild(hlBox);\n\n        function moveHighlight(el) {\n            if (!el || el === document.body) { hlBox.style.display = 'none'; return; }\n            var r = el.getBoundingClientRect();\n            hlBox.style.cssText = hlBox.style.cssText; // keep existing\n            hlBox.style.display = 'block';\n            hlBox.style.left  = (r.left   - 2) + 'px';\n            hlBox.style.top   = (r.top    - 2) + 'px';\n            hlBox.style.width = (r.width  + 4) + 'px';\n            hlBox.style.height= (r.height + 4) + 'px';\n        }\n\n        // Popover\n        var pop = document.createElement('div');\n        pop.style.cssText = [\n            'position:fixed', 'z-index:9999', 'display:none', 'width:300px',\n            'background:#1e293b', 'border:1px solid #3b82f6', 'border-radius:8px',\n            'padding:12px', 'box-shadow:0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,.5)',\n            'font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,\"Segoe UI\",Roboto,sans-serif'\n        ].join(';');\n        pop.innerHTML = [\n            '<div id=\"atn-sel\" style=\"font-size:11px;color:#64748b;margin-bottom:6px;',\n                'font-family:monospace;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap\"></div>',\n            '<textarea {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_d7bf7298eb3a4d10acfa814e","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t/*\n2\t * ADC Canvas — rendering helpers.\n3\t *\n4\t * Pure functions that turn a /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics card dict into a DOM\n5\t * topic-card element, and that build the four BUILT-IN card families the plan\n6\t * ships as fixed templates in this served frontend (Component Library →\n7\t * \"Built-in cards\"): (1) generic fallback, (2) first-run welcome card, (3)\n8\t * pending/ack cards (submit-time placeholder, per-thread pending with\n9\t * per-source progress, aged-pending treatment), and (4) the error/clarification\n10\t * family from Degraded-State UX. They are kept in this separate, loadable\n11\t * module (rather than inline in index.html) so the render contract can be\n12\t * verified headlessly — tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js and\n13\t * tests/e2e/canvas_builtin_runner.js load this exact file under a minimal DOM\n14\t * shim and assert the rendered HTML, with no browser or network required.\n15\t *\n16\t * ESCAPING CONTRACT (the render-path escaping contract, see plan UI-Regen Agent\n17\t * \"Escaping contract\"): every dynamic value — registry descriptions, utterance\n18\t * text, per-source names, error detail, elapsed strings, LLM/worker-authored\n19\t * free text — is interpolated through escapeHtml(), which sets it via a text\n20\t * node (textContent → the browser's own escaper). No dynamic value is ever\n21\t * spliced raw into innerHTML. The built-in builders below all go through the\n22\t * same escapeHtml() the topic card uses, so they are bound by the identical\n23\t * escaping contract.\n24\t *\n25\t * In the browser, index.html loads this via <script src=\"/canvas.js\"> before\n26\t * the inline app script, so these declarations are globals just like they were\n27\t * when they lived inline. In Node, the trailing export shim exposes them via\n28\t * module.exports for the DOM tests.\n29\t */\n30\t\n31\t// Node.js compatibility: ensure document is available in Node for headless tests\n32\t// When loaded in Node (module.exports exists), make the global document accessible\n33\t// to the el() helper function which uses document.createElement() and .createTextNode()\n34\tif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports && typeof document === 'undefined') {\n35\t    var document = global.document;\n36\t}\n37\t\n38\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n39\t// Tunables (exported so tests can assert against the same constants)\n40\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n41\t\n42\t// Hot-path intents flag at 30s pending (their budget is 3s) — see plan, The\n43\t// Async Path → Visible aging + Degraded-State UX aged-pending row. This flag is\n44\t// applied PURELY client-side from the local placeholder's creation time so it\n45\t// survives a hung/wedged server (no SSE dependency).\n46\tvar PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS = 30000;\n47\t\n48\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n49\t// Existing topic-card render helpers\n50\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n51\t\n52\tfunction formatStaleness(seconds) {\n53\t    if (seconds < 60) return 'just now';\n54\t    if (seconds < 3600) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 60)}m ago`;\n55\t    if (seconds < 86400) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 3600)}h ago`;\n56\t    return `${Math.floor(seconds / 86400)}d ago`;\n57\t}\n58\t\n59\tfunction getStalenessLevel(seconds) {\n60\t    if (seconds < 600) return 'fresh';      // 10 minutes\n61\t    if (seconds < 3600) return 'stale';     // 1 hour\n62\t    return 'very-stale';\n63\t}\n64\t\n65\t/**\n66\t * Build a topic card from topic data.\n67\t *\n68\t * @param {TopicCardData} cardData - Topic card data structure\n69\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .topic-card element\n70\t */\n71\tfunction createTopicCard(cardData) {\n72\t    const topic = cardData.topic;\n73\t    const staleness = cardData.staleness;\n74\t    const latestResult = cardData.latest_result;\n75\t\n76\t    const card = document.createElement('div');\n77\t    card.className = 'topic-card';\n78\t    card.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n79\t\n80\t    const stalenessLevel = getStalenessLevel(staleness.seconds);\n81\t    card.classList.add(stalenessLevel);\n82\t\n83\t    const typeClass = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n84\t\n85\t    // Expose the topic type as a data attribute alongside data-topic-id so cards\n86\t    // are queryable by type with a robust, stable selector (e.g.\n87\t    // [data-topic-type=\"research\"]) rather than relying on class names. The DOM\n88\t    // verification suites assert both dataset attributes are present per card.\n89\t    card.dataset.topicType = typeClass;\n90\t\n91\t    let html = `\n92\t        <div class=\"topic-header\">\n93\t            <div class=\"topic-label\">${escapeHtml(topic.label)}</div>\n94\t            <div>\n95\t                <span class=\"topic-type ${typeClass}\">${escapeHtml(topic.type || 'adhoc')}</span>\n96\t                ${stalenessLevel !== 'fresh' ? `<span class=\"stale-badge ${stalenessLevel}\">STALE</span>` : ''}\n97\t            </div>\n98\t        </div>\n99\t    `;\n100\t\n101\t    // Add latest result if available\n102\t    if (latestResult) {\n103\t        const urgencyClass = latestResult.urgency || 'normal';\n104\t        html += `\n105\t            <div class=\"result-content\">\n106\t                <div class=\"result-summary\">\n107\t                    ${escapeHtml(latestResult.summary)}\n108\t                    <span class=\"urgency-badge ${urgencyClass}\">${escapeHtml(urgencyClass)}</span>\n109\t                </div>\n110\t        `;\n111\t\n112\t        if (latestResult.data) {\n113\t            html += `<div class=\"result-data\">${escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(latestResult.data, null, 2))}</div>`;\n114\t        }\n115\t\n116\t        html += `</div>`;\n117\t    }\n118\t\n119\t    // Add staleness indicator\n120\t    const timeAgo = formatStaleness(staleness.seconds);\n121\t    html += `\n122\t        <div class=\"staleness-indicator ${stalenessLevel}\">\n123\t            <span class=\"staleness-dot ${stalenessLevel}\"></span>\n124\t            <span>Updated ${timeAgo}</span>\n125\t        </div>\n126\t    `;\n127\t\n128\t    card.innerHTML = html;\n129\t    return card;\n130\t}\n131\t\n132\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n133\t// Shared escaping + small DOM helpers (text-node based — escaping contract)\n134\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n135\t\n136\tfunction escapeHtml(text) {\n137\t    const div = document.createElement('div');\n138\t    div.textContent = text == null ? '' : String(text);\n139\t    return div.innerHTML;\n140\t}\n141\t\n142\t/**\n143\t * Build a DOM element with tag + className + a list of children/strings.\n144\t *\n145\t * Strings are appended as TEXT NODES (never spliced into innerHTML), which is\n146\t * the escaping contract's literal form — a markup-looking log line renders as\n147\t * literal text instead of breaking layout. Use this for any value that must be\n148\t * inserted verbatim as text.\n149\t */\n150\tfunction el(tag, className, children) {\n151\t    const node = document.createElement(tag);\n152\t    if (className) node.className = className;\n153\t    (children || []).forEach(function (c) {\n154\t        if (c == null) return;\n155\t        if (typeof c === 'string' || typeof c === 'number') {\n156\t            node.appendChild(document.createTextNode(String(c)));\n157\t        } else {\n158\t            node.appendChild(c);\n159\t        }\n160\t    });\n161\t    return node;\n162\t}\n163\t\n164\t// Seconds → \"0s\" / \"12s\" / \"1m 05s\" elapsed label for pending cards.\n165\tfunction formatElapsed(ms) {\n166\t    const s = Math.max(0, Math.floor((ms || 0) / 1000));\n167\t    if (s < 60) return s + 's';\n168\t    const m = Math.floor(s / 60);\n169\t    const rem = s % 60;\n170\t    return m + 'm ' + (rem < 10 ? '0' : '') + rem + 's';\n171\t}\n172\t\n173\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n174\t// (2) First-run welcome card — built-in, zero DB dependence\n175\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n176\t\n177\t// Default example utterances used when the registry yields no project with a\n178\t// supported intent to derive one from. These never touch the component DB.\n179\tvar DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES = [\n180\t    'Has the options pipeline caught up?',\n181\t    'What is the state of the ibkr mcp?',\n182\t    'Queue up a research task on recent pipeline errors — no rush.'\n183\t];\n184\t\n185\t// Map a supported intent type to an example utterance fragment, so the welcome\n186\t// card's examples are drawn from the projects the router actually knows about\n187\t// (plan: Cold start & demo seed — \"2-3 example utterances drawn from those\n188\t// projects' supported intents\").\n189\tvar INTENT_EXAMPLES = {\n190\t    status: 'What is the status of {proj}?',\n191\t    action: 'Restart the {proj} deployment.',\n192\t    brainstorm: 'Brainstorm trade-offs for {proj}.',\n193\t    lookup: 'Pull up recent logs for {proj}.',\n194\t    'task-profile': 'Queue up a research task on {proj} — no rush.',\n195\t    'self-modification': 'Add an alias to the {proj} registry entry.',\n196\t    'monitoring-config': 'Watch {proj} for pod restarts.',\n197\t    reminder: 'Remind me to check {proj} in an hour.'\n198\t};\n199\t\n200\tfunction _deriveExamples(projects) {\n201\t    const out = [];\n202\t    const seen = {};\n203\t    // Prefer projects that carry a description (registry.yaml entries) so the\n204\t    // welcome examples reference real, described projects — not bare discovered\n205\t    // repos with auto-README blurbs.\n206\t    const ordered = (projects || []).slice().sort(function (a, b) {\n207\t        const ad = a && a.description ? 0 : 1;\n208\t        const bd = b && b.description ? 0 : 1;\n209\t        return ad - bd;\n210\t    });\n211\t    ordered.forEach(function (p) {\n212\t        if (out.length >= 3) return;\n213\t        (p.intent_support || []).forEach(function (intent) {\n214\t            if (out.length >= 3 || seen[intent]) return;\n215\t            const tmpl = INTENT_EXAMPLES[intent];\n216\t            if (!tmpl) return;\n217\t            seen[intent] = true;\n218\t            out.push(tmpl.replace('{proj}', p.slug || p.name || 'the project'));\n219\t        });\n220\t    });\n221\t    return out.length ? out : DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES.slice(0, 3);\n222\t}\n223\t\n224\t// Cap the welcome-card project list so a 38-repo registry doesn't overwhelm the\n225\t// first frame. The first N are shown; an \"+N more\" note counts the rest.\n226\tvar WELCOME_PROJECT_CAP = 12;\n227\t\n228\t/**\n229\t * Build the first-run welcome card.\n230\t *\n231\t * @param {Array} projects  registry entries [{slug,name,description,intent_support,aliases}]\n232\t * @param {string} [description]  one-line description of aide-de-camp\n233\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .welcome-card element (data-builtin=\"welcome\")\n234\t *\n235\t * Renders even against an empty component library / DB — the project list comes\n236\t * from config/registry.yaml (served at /api/v1/registry), not the DB. The first\n237\t * real result replaces it (the canvas drops it once any topic card exists).\n238\t */\n239\tfunction createWelcomeCard(projects, description) {\n240\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card welcome-card');\n241\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'welcome';\n242\t\n243\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n244\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['👋']),\n245\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Welcome to ADC'])\n246\t    ]));\n247\t\n248\t    card.appendChild(el('p', 'builtin-desc', [\n249\t        description || 'A single input surface that routes what you say to the right project, fetches in parallel, and renders results as live cards.'\n250\t    ]));\n251\t\n252\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Registered projects']));\n253\t    const list = el('ul', 'builtin-project-list');\n254\t    (projects || []).forEach(function (p) {\n255\t        const slug = p.slug || p.name || 'project';\n256\t        const desc = p.description || '';\n257\t        const intents = (p.intent_support || []).join(' · ');\n258\t        const li = el('li', 'builtin-project');\n259\t        li.appendChild(el('strong', 'builtin-project-slug', [slug]));\n260\t        if (desc) li.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' — ' + desc));\n261\t        if (intents) li.appendChild(el('span', 'builtin-intents', [' ' + intents]));\n262\t        list.appendChild(li);\n263\t    });\n264\t    if (!(projects && projects.length)) {\n265\t        list.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-project', ['No projects registered yet.']));\n266\t    }\n267\t    card.appendChild(list);\n268\t\n269\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Try asking']));\n270\t    const examples = el('ul', 'builtin-examples');\n271\t    _deriveExamples(projects).forEach(function (ex) {\n272\t        examples.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-example', [ex]));\n273\t    });\n274\t    card.appendChild(examples);\n275\t\n276\t    return card;\n277\t}\n278\t\n279\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n280\t// Container decision — the headlessly-testable core of loadTopics()\n281\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n282\t\n283\t/**\n284\t * Decide what the canvas topic-container shows for a given card set.\n285\t *\n286\t * This is the shared render path index.html's ``loadTopics()`` calls after\n287\t * ``GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics``: on a fresh session with zero cards it\n288\t * shows the first-run welcome card (built from the registry project list — no\n289\t * DB/result dependence); the moment any real card exists it shows only topic\n290\t * cards, which drops any welcome card (never alongside real cards). The caller\n291\t * clears the container and appends the returned nodes.\n292\t *\n293\t * Kept here (not inline in index.html) so the zero→welcome / first-result→drop\n294\t * contract is verifiable headlessly: ``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js`` drives\n295\t * this exact function (``--container`` mode) and asserts the welcome card\n296\t * renders from an empty card set and is absent once a real card is present.\n297\t *\n298\t * @param {Array} [cards]      card dicts from /topics (empty → welcome card)\n299\t * @param {Array} [projects]   registry entries for the welcome card\n300\t * @param {string} [description] one-line description for the welcome card\n301\t * @returns {HTMLElement[]}    child nodes for the container\n302\t */\n303\tfunction buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description) {\n304\t    if (!cards || cards.length === 0) {\n305\t        return [createWelcomeCard(projects, description)];\n306\t    }\n307\t    return cards.map(function (c) { return createTopicCard(c); });\n308\t}\n309\t\n310\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n311\t// (3) Pending/ack cards — submit-time placeholder + per-thread pending\n312\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n313\t\n314\t/**\n315\t * Build a submit-time pending placeholder.\n316\t *\n317\t * Created LOCALLY at dispatch() submit time, BEFORE any server response, so a\n318\t * hung/wedged server still leaves a card on canvas to age (plan, Escalate\n319\t * Strand → Pending/ack card render path). It splits into per-thread pending\n320\t * cards when the dispatch ack (the /dispatch HTTP response) carries intent_ids.\n321\t *\n322\t * @param {string} utterance   the raw text being dispatched\n323\t * @param {number} createdAt   Date.now() at placeholder creation (ms epoch)\n324\t * @param {string} [pendingId] stable id for this placeholder (utterance id)\n325\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.placeholder (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n326\t */\n327\tfunction createPendingPlaceholderCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId) {\n328\t    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, 'Sending…');\n329\t    card.classList.add('placeholder');\n330\t    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'placeholder';\n331\t    return card;\n332\t}\n333\t\n334\t/**\n335\t * Build a per-thread pending card (one per dispatch-ack intent_id). Carries the\n336\t * per-source progress state ('3/5 sources in') and the elapsed-time footer.\n337\t *\n338\t * @param {string} intentId    the intent this thread tracks (result_created key)\n339\t * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance fragment/text\n340\t * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n341\t * @param {object} [progress]  {completed, total} initial per-source progress\n342\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.thread (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n343\t */\n344\tfunction createPendingThreadCard(intentId, utterance, createdAt, progress) {\n345\t    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, intentId, 'Working on it…');\n346\t    card.classList.add('thread');\n347\t    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'thread';\n348\t    if (progress) _setProgress(card, progress);\n349\t    return card;\n350\t}\n351\t\n352\t/**\n353\t * Split a submit-time placeholder into per-thread pending cards on the dispatch\n354\t * ack. The /dispatch ack response carries ``intent_ids`` — one per routed\n355\t * thread — so this produces one per-thread pending card per intent_id, each\n356\t * inheriting the placeholder's ``createdAt`` (so the 30s aged timer is\n357\t * continuous across the split — the timer started at submit, not at ack) and\n358\t * the originating utterance. (plan, Escalate Strand → Pending/ack card render\n359\t * path: \"splits into per-thread pending cards when the dispatch ack arrives\".)\n360\t *\n361\t * This is the headlessly-testable core of the split: index.html's dispatch()\n362\t * calls it with the ack's intent_ids and replaces the placeholder DOM node with\n363\t * the returned cards; the Node DOM shim exercises it directly (no browser) to\n364\t * prove the split is one-card-per-thread.\n365\t *\n366\t * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance text\n367\t * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n368\t * @param {string[]} intentIds the ack's intent_ids (one per routed thread)\n369\t * @returns {HTMLElement[]} one .pending-card.thread per intent_id\n370\t */\n371\tfunction splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds) {\n372\t    return (intentIds || []).map(function (id) {\n373\t        return createPendingThreadCard(id, utterance, createdAt);\n374\t    });\n375\t}\n376\t\n377\t// Shared skeleton for both placeholder + per-thread pending cards.\n378\tfunction _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, titleText) {\n379\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card pending-card');\n380\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'pending';\n381\t    card.dataset.createdAt = String(createdAt || 0);\n382\t    if (pendingId) card.dataset.pendingId = String(pendingId);\n383\t\n384\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n385\t        el('span', 'pending-spinner'),\n386\t        el('span', 'builtin-title pending-title', [titleText || 'Pending…'])\n387\t    ]));\n388\t\n389\t    if (utterance) {\n390\t        card.appendChild(el('p', 'pending-utterance', [utterance]));\n391\t    }\n392\t\n393\t    const progress = el('div', 'pending-progress');\n394\t    progress.style.display = 'none';\n395\t    card.appendChild(progress);\n396\t\n397\t    const elapsed = el('div', 'pending-elapsed', [\n398\t        formatElapsed(0) + ' elapsed'\n399\t    ]);\n400\t    card.appendChild(elapsed);\n401\t\n402\t    // Aged note (hidden until the 30s threshold) — see applyAgedTreatment().\n403\t    const agedNote = el('div', 'pending-aged-note');\n404\t    agedNote.style.display = 'none';\n405\t    agedNote.appendChild(document.createTextNode('Taking longer than expected — the server may be hung. '));\n406\t    const retry = el('button', 'pending-retry', ['Retry']);\n407\t    agedNote.appendChild(retry);\n408\t    card.appendChild(agedNote);\n409\t\n410\t    return card;\n411\t}\n412\t\n413\tfunction _setProgress(card, progress) {\n414\t    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-progress');\n415\t    if (!node || !progress) return;\n416\t    const completed = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.completed, 10) || 0);\n417\t    const total = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.total, 10) || 0);\n418\t    node.textContent = completed + '/' + total + ' sources in';\n419\t    node.dataset.completed = String(completed);\n420\t    node.dataset.total = String(total);\n421\t    node.style.display = total > 0 ? '' : 'none';\n422\t}\n423\t\n424\t/**\n425\t * Recompute the elapsed-time footer for a pending card against `now` (ms).\n426\t * Returns the elapsed ms. Pure: no timers, no globals beyond the card's own\n427\t * data-createdAt — so the headless harness can drive it via a mock clock.\n428\t */\n429\tfunction tickPendingElapsed(card, now) {\n430\t    const createdAt = parseInt(card.dataset.createdAt, 10) || 0;\n431\t    const elapsedMs = Math.max(0, (now || 0) - createdAt);\n432\t    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-elapsed');\n433\t    if (node) node.textContent = formatElapsed(elapsedMs) + ' elapsed';\n434\t    return elapsedMs;\n435\t}\n436\t\n437\t/**\n438\t * Apply (or remove) the aged-pending treatment based on elapsed ms vs the 30s\n439\t * threshold. Pure client-side — survives a hung server (no SSE dependency).\n440\t * Returns true if the card is currently flagged aged.\n441\t */\n442\tfunction applyAgedTreatment(card, now) {\n443\t    const elapsedMs = tickPendingElapsed(card, now);\n444\t    const aged = elapsedMs >= PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS;\n445\t    card.classList.toggle('aged', aged);\n446\t    const note = card.querySelector('.pending-aged-note');\n447\t    if (note) note.style.display = aged ? '' : 'none';\n448\t    return aged;\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n452\t// (4) Error / clarification family — Degraded-State UX, filled client-side\n453\t//     from SSE error events\n454\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n455\t\n456\t// Display metadata per error variant. title + icon + a one-line description\n457\t// template. Variants accept both snake_case and kebab-case keys (normalized in\n458\t// _errorVariant()). Maps directly to the Degraded-State UX matrix rows.\n459\tvar ERROR_VARIANTS = {\n460\t    router_unavailable: {\n461\t        title: 'Router unavailable',\n462\t        icon: '🔌',\n463\t        detail: 'The LLM proxy (ZAI) was unreachable at the router stage, so this utterance could not be routed.'\n464\t    },\n465\t    all_sources_failed: {\n466\t        title: 'No data available',\n467\t        icon: '📭',\n468\t        detail: 'Every fetch source failed or timed out, so there is nothing to synthesize.'\n469\t    },\n470\t    synthesis_failed: {\n471\t        title: 'Summary unavailable',\n472\t        icon: '⚠️',\n473\t        detail: 'The raw data was fetched, but the summary could not be produced.'\n474\t    },\n475\t    malformed_router_output: {\n476\t        title: \"Couldn't parse that into intents\",\n477\t        icon: '🧩',\n478\t        detail: 'The router returned output the system could not interpret.'\n479\t    },\n480\t    no_match: {\n481\t        title: 'No matching project',\n482\t        icon: '❓',\n483\t        detail: 'No registered project matched this request.'\n484\t    }\n485\t};\n486\t\n487\tfunction _normalizeVariant(errorType) {\n488\t    if (!errorType) return 'no_match';\n489\t    const key = String(errorType).trim().replace(/-/g, '_').toLowerCase();\n490\t    return ERROR_VARIANTS[key] ? key : 'no_match';\n491\t}\n492\t\n493\t/**\n494\t * Build an error/clarification card from an SSE error event payload.\n495\t *\n496\t * @param {ErrorCardData} data - Error card data structure\n497\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .error-card element (data-builtin=\"error\")\n498\t *\n499\t * Per-source failure lists, the echoed utterance, raw degraded data, and any\n500\t * detail text are all inserted as text nodes (escaping contract).\n501\t */\n502\tfunction createErrorCard(data) {\n503\t    data = data || {};\n504\t    const variant = _normalizeVariant(data.error_type);\n505\t    const meta = ERROR_VARIANTS[variant];\n506\t\n507\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card error-card error-' + variant);\n508\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'error';\n509\t    card.dataset.errorType = variant;\n510\t    if (data.intent_id) card.dataset.intentId = String(data.intent_id);\n511\t    if (data.pending_id) card.dataset.pendingId = String(data.pending_id);\n512\t\n513\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n514\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', [meta.icon]),\n515\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', [meta.title])\n516\t    ]));\n517\t\n518\t    const detail = data.detail || meta.detail;\n519\t    card.appendChild(el('p', 'error-detail', [detail]));\n520\t\n521\t    // Echo the utterance so nothing is ever lost (router_unavailable /\n522\t    // malformed_router_output rows). Rendered as text, never raw HTML.\n523\t    if (data.utterance) {\n524\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-utterance-wrap', [\n525\t            el('span', 'error-utterance-label', ['You said: ']),\n526\t            el('span', 'error-utterance', ['“' + data.utterance + '”'])\n527\t        ]));\n528\t    }\n529\t\n530\t    // Per-source failure list (all_sources_failed row).\n531\t    const sources = data.sources;\n532\t    if (Array.isArray(sources) && sources.length) {\n533\t        const ul = el('ul', 'error-source-list');\n534\t        sources.forEach(function (s) {\n535\t            const name = (s && (s.name || s.source)) || 'source';\n536\t            const reason = (s && (s.reason || s.error)) || 'failed';\n537\t            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-source', [\n538\t                el('span', 'error-source-name', [name + ': ']),\n539\t                el('span', 'error-source-reason', [reason])\n540\t            ]));\n541\t        });\n542\t        card.appendChild(ul);\n543\t    }\n544\t\n545\t    // Degraded raw data (synthesis_failed row): fetched data is never discarded.\n546\t    if (data.data != null) {\n547\t        const raw = typeof data.data === 'string' ? data.data : JSON.stringify(data.data, null, 2);\n548\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-raw-label', ['Fetched data']));\n549\t        card.appendChild(el('pre', 'error-raw', [raw]));\n550\t    }\n551\t\n552\t    // No-match clarification: list the registered projects so the user can pick.\n553\t    if (variant === 'no_match' && Array.isArray(data.registered_projects) && data.registered_projects.length) {\n554\t        const ul = el('ul', 'error-project-list');\n555\t        data.registered_projects.forEach(function (p) {\n556\t            const slug = typeof p === 'string' ? p : (p.slug || p.name || 'project');\n557\t            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-project', [slug]));\n558\t        });\n559\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-projects-wrap', [\n560\t            el('span', 'error-projects-label', ['Registered projects: ']),\n561\t            ul\n562\t        ]));\n563\t    }\n564\t\n565\t    // Recovery action button. Variant-aware label.\n566\t    const retryLabel = (variant === 'malformed_router_output' || variant === 'no_match')\n567\t        ? 'Edit & resend'\n568\t        : 'Retry';\n569\t    card.appendChild(el('button', 'error-retry', [retryLabel]));\n570\t\n571\t    return card;\n572\t}\n573\t\n574\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n575\t// Canvas Card Type Definitions\n576\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n577\t\n578\t/**\n579\t * @typedef {Object} StuckCardData\n580\t * @property {string} bead_id - } catch (e) { return [['value', data]]; }\n803\t    }\n804\t    if (Array.isArray(obj)) {\n805\t        return obj.slice(0, 50).map(function (v, i) { return [String(i), _stringify(v)]; });\n806\t    }\n807\t    if (typeof obj === 'object') {\n808\t        return Object.keys(obj).slice(0, 50).map(function (k) { return [k, _stringify(obj[k])]; });\n809\t    }\n810\t    return [['value', String(obj)]];\n811\t}\n812\t\n813\tfunction _stringify(v) {\n814\t    if (v == null) return '';\n815\t    if (typeof v === 'string') return v;\n816\t    if (typeof v === 'number' || typeof v === 'boolean') return String(v);\n817\t    try { return JSON.stringify(v); } catch (e) { return String(v); }\n818\t}\n819\t\n820\t// Export shim: browser globals are already in place (these are top-level\n821\t// function declarations).
